key_points: Hague House has reputation for chaos and disorder
Students ask about suites, Netherlands South, Bill of Rights Hall
Heat reliability and bathroom cleanliness are common concerns
key_quotes: I saw a post on here saying that Hague house is bad and there is a lot of chaos
Does the heat work all year long? How often are the bathrooms cleaned? Is it quiet?
My top choice is Netherlands south. Do you think you can tell me how the walk is to the major academic buildings?

key_points: Greek life exists but not mandatory to have a social life
Commuters worry about making friends; dorming helps connection
Weekend life and party scene are unclear from official sources
key_quotes: Do you have to join a sorority to be able to find life and have fun?
I hope to be transferring to hofstra next year... I was curious what the weekend life is like?
Is there a lot to do in terms of parties and actual life besides clubs?

key_points: Almost no student commentary on food quality or variety
On-campus food options not prominently discussed
Students more concerned about textbook costs than dining
key_quotes: What kind of food is available on campus?
I don't wanna pay $350 for a couple of books bro
On campus jobs don't even pay minimum wage

key_points: One student failed all classes freshman year and nearly lost scholarship
Scholarship GPA requirements create high academic stress
No specific mentions of counseling services or mental health support
key_quotes: My first year was honestly really bad... my overall gpa for my first year is probably a 2.0
I got called in by my advisor saying that I might have to make an appeal for my scholarship if I couldn't bring my gpa to a 2.8
I feel so overwhelmed... I can't shake that feeling of being overwhelmed

key_points: Merit scholarships range from $17k-$37k+ per year, but sticker price is ~$45-62k
Financial aid office is slow to respond and confusing for international students
GPA requirements to maintain scholarships are strict; students lose aid easily
key_quotes: Hofstra costs me like 40k a year, Im a freshman so my resume is trash & I have no car, and on campus jobs don't even pay minimum wage
I got a conditional acceptance... she never responds with the answer I want no matter how many emails I send
I just got the email regarding my acceptance but the amount I'm gonna pay is over what me and my family can achieve

key_points: Orgo professor Dr. Tim Schleif praised highly by students
Engineering program quality questioned; unclear if competitive
Nursing program is newer, comparisons to Adelphi common
key_quotes: DR. TIM SCHLEIF IS THE BEST ORGO PROF AT HOFSTRAAAAAA - he is very kind, funny and extremely helpful
Hows the engineering in hofstra? Ignore price for now - is hofstra genuinely a good school for engineering?
My only concern is how new the Hofstra nursing program is. Is it on par with the experience in Adelphi?

key_points: VP Jessica Eads accused of serious HR misconduct by affiliated insider
AI-read graduation names sparked petition from angry students
Admissions AO noted for writing sticky notes revealing essay topics on physical acceptance letters
key_quotes: This Administration (namely VP Eads) is running this school into the ground... AT LEAST 4 instances of employees under her who are a woman, a minority, or both, being placed on leave
Hey guys, not sure if everyone heard about our names being read by AI at graduation... This bothered me, so I decided to make a petition
If you get in, their ao will often write you a sticky note about how they liked your essay and they will mention the topic of your essay

key_points: Campus is widely considered beautiful; scenic and well-maintained
Proximity to NYC marketed heavily but shuttle to LIRR is unreliable
Hempstead area surrounding campus is considered unsafe by some
key_quotes: They literally can't shut up about how they're jUsT aN hOuR aWaY fRoM nYc
I take the LIRR to mineola and have waited long stretches of time for a shuttle i'm starting to believe doesn't exist
I toured the campus a few months ago and found everything to be pretty nice, especially the scenery and the Science and Innovation Center

key_points: Zarb business school has Big 4 accounting recruiting pathways
NYC proximity helps with internships but not guaranteed
University representatives made bold co-op and job conversion claims to international students
key_quotes: The university representatives said that I can recover the cost with guaranteed scholarships and co-op programs worth at least $42000. Also, the rate of converting your internships to your full-time job is 75%
Would you say its proximity to the city has advantages for internships and other opportunities?
I'm an accounting major and looking to land a job at Big 4. I'm not sure if Hofstra University, Pace University, or Baruch College is a better choice

key_points: Sticker price ~$45-62k/year; even with scholarships net cost is 20-35k
Students frequently compare Hofstra unfavorably to cheaper SUNY options
On-campus jobs pay below minimum wage, making debt repayment harder
key_quotes: I applied to Hofstra because they kept spamming my inbox with fee waivers... They even threw in a 17k/yr scholarship to put a dent on their 45k yearly price tag
How should I start making some money guys, idk what to do. Hofstra costs me like 40k a year
I calculated my aid and all that after scholarships and my estimated pay each year is 20-23k. I don't know if I could afford that

key_points: Hofstra is a commuter-heavy school with a modest residential culture
Strong sense that it's a backup school; many students stumbled into it
Campus is pleasant but student life energy is lukewarm
key_quotes: I've read it's essentially a commuter school
I spontaneously applied, forgot I applied, and got in. I don't want to miss a good opportunity of going to a good school just because it's not elite
I am currently a student at Hofstra University, and this was my safety school

key_points: **Administration:** VP-level HR misconduct allegations involving minority and female employees
**Privacy:** AO writes essay topic details on physical acceptance letters mailed home
**Admissions:** Aggressive spam emails with fake 'select candidate' status sent to virtually all students
key_quotes: There's AT LEAST 4 instances of employees under her who are a woman, a minority, or both, being placed on leave
Please be careful. If you get in, their ao will often write you a sticky note... they will mention the topic of your essay
FrOsTyLyNx, BeCaUsE oF yOuR sElEcT cAnDiDaTe StAtUs... [mockery of Hofstra's mass spam emails]

key_points: On-site interviews can yield surprise $25k/year merit scholarships
Science and Innovation Center is genuinely impressive facility
Dual-degree PA and JD programs offer accelerated professional tracks
key_quotes: At the actual session I was not only accepted, but given a VERY generous 25k a year scholarship to Hofstra. I wasn't planning on even considering Hofstra prior to this
I toured the campus a few months ago and found everything to be pretty nice, especially the scenery and the Science and Innovation Center
I'm a prospective first-year student and would like to know if Hofstra is worth the time and money... especially the dual degree PA program
